prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_rf', seasonality_champion='CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: FT_rf dominates as overall champion with lowest RMSE (3000.78) and best trend tracking (slope err: 0.65). High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 3.60) and high seasonality variance (0.69) indicate models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting horizon-specific selection could help. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating individual weaknesses.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "FT_rf", "late": "ONLY_FT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_rf", "rf", "DWT_rf", "ETS", "THETA", "FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 3.5975,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "FT_rf dominates as overall champion with lowest RMSE (3000.78) and best trend tracking (slope err: 0.65). High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 3.60) and high seasonality variance (0.69) indicate models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting horizon-specific selection could help. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.62 (>0.5 threshold) justifies robust combination approaches like dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). FT_rf dominates as overall champion (RMSE=4010.27) but high disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=3.60) means we need diverse methods. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 adds selection diversity while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides weighted averaging with shrinkage to handle few windows (n_windows=3). score_preset='rmse_focus' aligns with high model dispersion."
}

High model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio: 3.60) and high seasonality variance (0.69) indicate models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting horizon-specific selection could help. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors while mitigating individual weaknesses.
